title: Hypertrophic scar review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Supports distinguishing mature scars, hypertrophic scars, keloids, and mimics; documenting scar growth within or beyond the original wound, shape, elevation, redness, hardness, progression, risk context, visual inspection, palpation, and diagnostic uncertainty.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact scar diagnostic section supports documenting a possible or established hypertrophic scar, its onset and progression, growth relative to the original wound, location and clinician-observed morphology without assuming that every raised scar is hypertrophic.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports distinguishing mature scars, hypertrophic scars, keloids, and mimics; documenting scar growth within or beyond the original wound, shape, elevation, redness, hardness, progression, risk context, visual inspection, palpation, and diagnostic uncertainty.

source.title: Diagnosis and Treatment of Keloids and Hypertrophic Scars—Japan Scar Workshop Consensus Document 2018

exact_section.heading: Diagnostic algorithm for pathological scars and clinical diagnosis of keloids and hypertrophic scars
exact_section.locator: publisher full text, Main context, lines 132–186

referral.text: Supports recording a clinician-established scar type, subjective symptoms, lesion location, severity context, prior response, movement or tension context, and completed specialist assessment without mapping any treatment instruction.
referral.rationale: The exact assessment points support recording clinician-assessed severity, subjective symptoms, functional effect, lesion location and prior response; no score, diagnosis, treatment, or referral is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports recording a clinician-established scar type, subjective symptoms, lesion location, severity context, prior response, movement or tension context, and completed specialist assessment without mapping any treatment instruction.

source.title: Diagnosis and Treatment of Keloids and Hypertrophic Scars—Japan Scar Workshop Consensus Document 2018

exact_section.heading: Treatment algorithms at different medical facilities — assessment before treatment

sources_used.jurisdiction: Japan; international scar-consensus evidence requiring UAE adaptation

source_limitations: Original injury or procedure, exact date, measurements, colour, thickness, firmness, pruritus, pain, bleeding, ulceration, discharge, inflammation, contracture, movement restriction, family and prior-scar history, wound complications, prior treatment, photography, investigations, diagnosis, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported unless separately clinician documented.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
